1	from contextlib import AsyncExitStack
2	
3	from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send
4	
5	
6	# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
7	# in their own AsyncExitStack
8	class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
9	    def __init__(
10	        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
11	    ) -> None:
12	        self.app = app
13	        self.context_name = context_name
14	
15	    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
16	        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
17	            scope[self.context_name] = stack
18	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
19

1	from collections.abc import Callable, Mapping
2	from typing import (
3	    Annotated,
4	    Any,
5	    BinaryIO,
6	    TypeVar,
7	    cast,
8	)
9	
10	from annotated_doc import Doc
11	from pydantic import GetJsonSchemaHandler
12	from starlette.datastructures import URL as URL  # noqa: F401
13	from starlette.datastructures import Address as Address  # noqa: F401
14	from starlette.datastructures import FormData as FormData  # noqa: F401
15	from starlette.datastructures import Headers as Headers  # noqa: F401
16	from starlette.datastructures import QueryParams as QueryParams  # noqa: F401
17	from starlette.datastructures import State as State  # noqa: F401
18	from starlette.datastructures import UploadFile as StarletteUploadFile
19	
20	
21	class UploadFile(StarletteUploadFile):
22	    """
23	    A file uploaded in a request.
24	
25	    Define it as a *path operation function* (or dependency) parameter.
26	
27	    If you are using a regular `def` function, you can use the `upload_file.file`
28	    attribute to access the raw standard Python file (blocking, not async), useful and
29	    needed for non-async code.
30	
31	    Read more about it in the
32	    [FastAPI docs for Request Files](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/request-files/).
33	
34	    ## Example
35	
36	    ```python
37	    from typing import Annotated
38	
39	    from fastapi import FastAPI, File, UploadFile
40	
41	    app = FastAPI()
42	
43	
44	    @app.post("/files/")
45	    async def create_file(file: Annotated[bytes, File()]):
46	        return {"file_size": len(file)}
47	
48	
49	    @app.post("/uploadfile/")
50	    async def create_upload_file(file: UploadFile):
51	        return {"filename": file.filename}
52	    ```
53	    """
54	
55	    file: Annotated[
56	        BinaryIO,
57	        Doc("The standard Python file object (non-async)."),
58	    ]
59	    filename: Annotated[str | None, Doc("The original file name.")]
60	    size: Annotated[int | None, Doc("The size of the file in bytes.")]
61	    headers: Annotated[Headers, Doc("The headers of the request.")]
62	    content_type: Annotated[
63	        str | None, Doc("The content type of the request, from the headers.")
64	    ]
65	
66	    async def write(
67	        self,
68	        data: Annotated[
69	            bytes,
70	            Doc(
71	                """
72	                The bytes to write to the file.
73	                """
74	            ),
75	        ],
76	    ) -> None:
77	        """
78	        Write some bytes to the file.
79	
80	        You normally wouldn't use this from a file you read in a request.
81	
82	        To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.
83	        """
84	        return await super().write(data)
85	
86	    async def read(
87	        self,
88	        size: Annotated[
89	            int,
90	            Doc(
91	                """
92	                The number of bytes to read from the file.
93	                """
94	            ),
95	        ] = -1,
96	    ) -> bytes:
97	        """
98	        Read some bytes from the file.
99	
100	        To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.
101	        """
102	        return await super().read(size)
103	
104	    async def seek(
105	        self,
106	        offset: Annotated[
107	            int,
108	            Doc(
109	                """
110	                The position in bytes to seek to in the file.
111	                """
112	            ),
113	        ],
114	    ) -> None:
115	        """
116	        Move to a position in the file.
117	
118	        Any next read or write will be done from that position.
119	
120	        To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.
121	        """
122	        return await super().seek(offset)
123	
124	    async def close(self) -> None:
125	        """
126	        Close the file.
127	
128	        To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.
129	        """
130	        return await super().close()
131	
132	    @classmethod
133	    def _validate(cls, __input_value: Any, _: Any) -> "UploadFile":
134	        if not isinstance(__input_value, StarletteUploadFile):
135	            raise ValueError(f"Expected UploadFile, received: {type(__input_value)}")
136	        return cast(UploadFile, __input_value)
137	
138	    @classmethod
139	    def __get_pydantic_json_schema__(
140	        cls, core_schema: Mapping[str, Any], handler: GetJsonSchemaHandler
141	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
142	        return {"type": "string", "contentMediaType": "application/octet-stream"}
143	
144	    @classmethod
145	    def __get_pydantic_core_schema__(
146	        cls, source: type[Any], handler: Callable[[Any], Mapping[str, Any]]
147	    ) -> Mapping[str, Any]:
148	        from ._compat.v2 import with_info_plain_validator_function
149	
150	        return with_info_plain_validator_function(cls._validate)
151

153	class DefaultPlaceholder:
154	    """
155	    You shouldn't use this class directly.
156	
157	    It's used internally to recognize when a default value has been overwritten, even
158	    if the overridden default value was truthy.
159	    """
160	
161	    def __init__(self, value: Any):
162	        self.value = value
163	
164	    def __bool__(self) -> bool:
165	        return bool(self.value)
166	
167	    def __eq__(self, o: object) -> bool:
168	        return isinstance(o, DefaultPlaceholder) and o.value == self.value
169	
170	
171	DefaultType = TypeVar("DefaultType")
172	
173	
174	def Default(value: DefaultType) -> DefaultType:
175	    """
176	    You shouldn't use this function directly.
177	
178	    It's used internally to recognize when a default value has been overwritten, even
179	    if the overridden default value was truthy.
180	    """
181	    return DefaultPlaceholder(value)  # type: ignore
182	
183	
184	# Sentinel for "parameter not provided" in Param/FieldInfo.
185	# Typed as None to satisfy ty
186	_Unset = Default(None)

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

45	class FastAPI(Starlette):
46	    """
47	    `FastAPI` app class, the main entrypoint to use FastAPI.
48	
49	    Read more in the
50	    [FastAPI docs for First Steps](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/first-steps/).
51	
52	    ## Example
53	
54	    ```python
55	    from fastapi import FastAPI
56	
57	    app = FastAPI()
58	    ```
59	    """
60	
61	    def __init__(
62	        self: AppType,
63	        *,
64	        debug: Annotated[
65	            bool,
66	            Doc(
67	                """
68	                Boolean indicating if debug tracebacks should be returned on server
69	                errors.
70	
71	                Read more in the
72	                [Starlette docs for Applications](https://www.starlette.dev/applications/#instantiating-the-application).
73	                """
74	            ),
75	        ] = False,
76	        routes: Annotated[
77	            list[BaseRoute] | None,
78	            Doc(
79	                """
80	                **Note**: you probably shouldn't use this parameter, it is inherited
81	                from Starlette and supported for compatibility.
82	
83	                ---
84	
85	                A list of routes to serve incoming HTTP and WebSocket requests.
86	                """
87	            ),
88	            deprecated(
89	                """
90	                You normally wouldn't use this parameter with FastAPI, it is inherited
91	                from Starlette and supported for compatibility.
92	
93	                In FastAPI, you normally would use the *path operation methods*,
94	                like `app.get()`, `app.post()`, etc.
95	                """
96	            ),
97	        ] = None,
98	        title: Annotated[
99	            str,
100	            Doc(
101	                """
102	                The title of the API.
103	
104	                It will be added to the generated OpenAPI (e.g. visible at `/docs`).
105	
106	                Read more in the
107	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-api).
108	
109	                **Example**
110	
111	                ```python
112	                from fastapi import FastAPI
113	
114	                app = FastAPI(title="ChimichangApp")
115	                ```
116	                """
117	            ),
118	        ] = "FastAPI",
119	        summary: Annotated[
120	            str | None,
121	            Doc(
122	                """
123	                A short summary of the API.
124	
125	                It will be added to the generated OpenAPI (e.g. visible at `/docs`).
126	
127	                Read more in the
128	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-api).
129	
130	                **Example**
131	
132	                ```python
133	                from fastapi import FastAPI
134	
135	                app = FastAPI(summary="Deadpond's favorite app. Nuff said.")
136	                ```
137	                """
138	            ),
139	        ] = None,
140	        description: Annotated[
141	            str,
142	            Doc(
143	                '''
144	                A description of the API. Supports Markdown (using
145	                [CommonMark syntax](https://commonmark.org/)).
146	
147	                It will be added to the generated OpenAPI (e.g. visible at `/docs`).
148	
149	                Read more in the
150	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-api).
151	
152	                **Example**
153	
154	                ```python
155	                from fastapi import FastAPI
156	
157	                app = FastAPI(
158	                    description="""
159	                                ChimichangApp API helps you do awesome stuff. 🚀
160	
161	                                ## Items
162	
163	                                You can **read items**.
164	
165	                                ## Users
166	
167	                                You will be able to:
168	
169	                                * **Create users** (_not implemented_).
170	                                * **Read users** (_not implemented_).
171	
172	                                """
173	                )
174	                ```
175	                '''
176	            ),
177	        ] = "",
178	        version: Annotated[
179	            str,
180	            Doc(
181	                """
182	                The version of the API.
183	
184	                **Note** This is the version of your application, not the version of
185	                the OpenAPI specification nor the version of FastAPI being used.
186	
187	                It will be added to the generated OpenAPI (e.g. visible at `/docs`).
188	
189	                Read more in the
190	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-api).
191	
192	                **Example**
193	
194	                ```python
195	                from fastapi import FastAPI
196	
197	                app = FastAPI(version="0.0.1")
198	                ```
199	                """
200	            ),
201	        ] = "0.1.0",
202	        openapi_url: Annotated[
203	            str | None,
204	            Doc(
205	                """
206	                The URL where the OpenAPI schema will be served from.
207	
208	                If you set it to `None`, no OpenAPI schema will be served publicly, and
209	                the default automatic endpoints `/docs` and `/redoc` will also be
210	                disabled.
211	
212	                Read more in the
213	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#openapi-url).
214	
215	                **Example**
216	
217	                ```python
218	                from fastapi import FastAPI
219	
220	                app = FastAPI(openapi_url="/api/v1/openapi.json")
221	                ```
222	                """
223	            ),
224	        ] = "/openapi.json",
225	        openapi_tags: Annotated[
226	            list[dict[str, Any]] | None,
227	            Doc(
228	                """
229	                A list of tags used by OpenAPI, these are the same `tags` you can set
230	                in the *path operations*, like:
231	
232	                * `@app.get("/users/", tags=["users"])`
233	                * `@app.get("/items/", tags=["items"])`
234	
235	                The order of the tags can be used to specify the order shown in
236	                tools like Swagger UI, used in the automatic path `/docs`.
237	
238	                It's not required to specify all the tags used.
239	
240	                The tags that are not declared MAY be organized randomly or based
241	                on the tools' logic. Each tag name in the list MUST be unique.
242	
243	                The value of each item is a `dict` containing:
244	
245	                * `name`: The name of the tag.
246	                * `description`: A short description of the tag.
247	                    [CommonMark syntax](https://commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich
248	                    text representation.
249	                * `externalDocs`: Additional external documentation for this tag. If
250	                    provided, it would contain a `dict` with:
251	                    * `description`: A short description of the target documentation.
252	                        [CommonMark syntax](https://commonmark.org/) MAY be used for
253	                        rich text representation.
254	                    * `url`: The URL for the target documentation. Value MUST be in
255	                        the form of a URL.
256	
257	                Read more in the
258	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-tags).
259	
260	                **Example**
261	
262	                ```python
263	                from fastapi import FastAPI

1023	        # Duplicate/override from Starlette to add AsyncExitStackMiddleware
1024	        # inside of ExceptionMiddleware, inside of custom user middlewares

1032	            else:

1034	
1035	        middleware = (

1037	            + self.user_middleware
1038	            + [

1040	                    ExceptionMiddleware,  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1041	                    handlers=exception_handlers,
1042	                    debug=debug,

1044	                # Add FastAPI-specific AsyncExitStackMiddleware for closing files.
1045	                # Before this was also used for closing dependencies with yield but
1046	                # those now have their own AsyncExitStack, to properly support
1047	                # streaming responses while keeping compatibility with the previous
1048	                # versions (as of writing 0.117.1) that allowed doing
1049	                # except HTTPException inside a dependency with yield.
1050	                # This needs to happen after user middlewares because those create a
1051	                # new contextvars context copy by using a new AnyIO task group.
1052	                # This AsyncExitStack preserves the context for contextvars, not
1053	                # strictly necessary for closing files but it was one of the original
1054	                # intentions.
1055	                # If the AsyncExitStack lived outside of the custom middlewares and
1056	                # contextvars were set, for example in a dependency with 'yield'
1057	                # in that internal contextvars context, the values would not be
1058	                # available in the outer context of the AsyncExitStack.
1059	                # By placing the middleware and the AsyncExitStack here, inside all
1060	                # user middlewares, the same context is used.
1061	                # This is currently not needed, only for closing files, but used to be
1062	                # important when dependencies with yield were closed here.

1064	            ]

12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54

1022	    def build_middleware_stack(self) -> ASGIApp:
1023	        # Duplicate/override from Starlette to add AsyncExitStackMiddleware
1024	        # inside of ExceptionMiddleware, inside of custom user middlewares
1025	        debug = self.debug
1026	        error_handler = None
1027	        exception_handlers: dict[Any, ExceptionHandler] = {}
1028	
1029	        for key, value in self.exception_handlers.items():
1030	            if key in (500, Exception):
1031	                error_handler = value
1032	            else:
1033	                exception_handlers[key] = value
1034	
1035	        middleware = (
1036	            [Middleware(ServerErrorMiddleware, handler=error_handler, debug=debug)]  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1037	            + self.user_middleware
1038	            + [
1039	                Middleware(
1040	                    ExceptionMiddleware,  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1041	                    handlers=exception_handlers,
1042	                    debug=debug,
1043	                ),
1044	                # Add FastAPI-specific AsyncExitStackMiddleware for closing files.
1045	                # Before this was also used for closing dependencies with yield but
1046	                # those now have their own AsyncExitStack, to properly support
1047	                # streaming responses while keeping compatibility with the previous
1048	                # versions (as of writing 0.117.1) that allowed doing
1049	                # except HTTPException inside a dependency with yield.
1050	                # This needs to happen after user middlewares because those create a
1051	                # new contextvars context copy by using a new AnyIO task group.
1052	                # This AsyncExitStack preserves the context for contextvars, not
1053	                # strictly necessary for closing files but it was one of the original
1054	                # intentions.
1055	                # If the AsyncExitStack lived outside of the custom middlewares and
1056	                # contextvars were set, for example in a dependency with 'yield'
1057	                # in that internal contextvars context, the values would not be
1058	                # available in the outer context of the AsyncExitStack.
1059	                # By placing the middleware and the AsyncExitStack here, inside all
1060	                # user middlewares, the same context is used.
1061	                # This is currently not needed, only for closing files, but used to be
1062	                # important when dependencies with yield were closed here.
1063	                Middleware(AsyncExitStackMiddleware),  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1064	            ]
1065	        )
1066	
1067	        app = self.router
1068	        for cls, args, kwargs in reversed(middleware):
1069	            app = cls(app, *args, **kwargs)

content.text:
Perfect! I can see that FastAPI inherits from Starlette. Let me create a comprehensive report based on all the information I've gathered:

## Comprehensive FastAPI Middleware Directory Analysis

### 1. **Directory Contents**

The `/fastapi/middleware/` directory contains the following files:

- `__init__.py` - Package initialization
- `cors.py` - CORS middleware
- `gzip.py` - GZip compression middleware
- `httpsredirect.py` - HTTPS redirect middleware
- `trustedhost.py` - Trusted host middleware
- `wsgi.py` - WSGI middleware
- `asyncexitstack.py` - FastAPI-specific async exit stack middleware

### 2. **File Contents**

#### `__init__.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware
```
This file simply re-exports Starlette's Middleware class, making it available directly from `fastapi.middleware`.

#### `cors.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware as CORSMiddleware  # noqa
```
Direct re-export of Starlette's CORSMiddleware, allowing cross-origin resource sharing configuration.

#### `gzip.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.gzip import GZipMiddleware as GZipMiddleware  # noqa
```
Direct re-export of Starlette's GZipMiddleware for response compression.

#### `httpsredirect.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.httpsredirect import (  # noqa
    HTTPSRedirectMiddleware as HTTPSRedirectMiddleware,
)
```
Direct re-export of Starlette's HTTPSRedirectMiddleware for enforcing HTTPS connections.

#### `trustedhost.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.trustedhost import (  # noqa
    TrustedHostMiddleware as TrustedHostMiddleware,
)
```
Direct re-export of Starlette's TrustedHostMiddleware for validating Host headers.

#### `wsgi.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.wsgi import (
    WSGIMiddleware as WSGIMiddleware,
)  # pragma: no cover # noqa
```
Direct re-export of Starlette's WSGIMiddleware for mounting WSGI applications.

#### `asyncexitstack.py` (FastAPI-specific)
```python
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack

from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send


# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
# in their own AsyncExitStack
class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(
        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
    ) -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

This is the **only custom middleware** in FastAPI. It provides a mechanism to manage async context cleanup (primarily for closing files) after a request is completed. It stores an `AsyncExitStack` in the ASGI scope under the key `"fastapi_middleware_astack"`.

### 3. **Middleware Structuring in FastAPI**

Based on the `applications.py` analysis, FastAPI implements middleware as follows:

**Middleware Stack Order (from outermost to innermost):**
1. `ServerErrorMiddleware` - Catches unhandled errors
2. User-provided middleware (via `add_middleware()` or `@app.middleware()`)
3. `ExceptionMiddleware` - Handles FastAPI/Starlette exceptions
4. `AsyncExitStackMiddleware` - Manages async resource cleanup (FastAPI-specific)
5. Router (actual route handlers)

**Key Pattern:**
- Middleware is built in `build_middleware_stack()` method
- Middleware follows the ASGI pattern: `async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send)`
- Middleware wraps applications in reverse order: `for cls, args, kwargs in reversed(middleware):`

### 4. **BaseHTTPMiddleware Pattern**

FastAPI uses Starlette's `BaseHTTPMiddleware` through two mechanisms:

**Via Decorator:**
```python
@app.middleware("http")
async def add_process_time_header(
    request: Request, call_next: Callable[[Request], Awaitable[Response]]
) -> Response:
    start_time = time.time()
    response = await call_next(request)
    process_time = time.time() - start_time
    response.headers["X-Process-Time"] = str(process_time)
    return response
```

**Via Method:**
```python
app.add_middleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware, dispatch=func)
```

The `middleware()` decorator internally converts the request/response signature to `BaseHTTPMiddleware` format and adds it to the middleware stack.

### 5. **FastAPI Architecture Insights**

- **Inheritance**: FastAPI extends Starlette, inheriting the `add_middleware()` method
- **Custom Addition**: FastAPI adds `AsyncExitStackMiddleware` specifically for resource cleanup
- **Context Preservation**: The AsyncExitStackMiddleware placement inside user middlewares preserves contextvars context
- **Re-exports**: All standard Starlette middleware is made available through FastAPI's namespace for convenience

All middleware must implement the ASGI interface (`__call__` method accepting scope, receive, send parameters).

Perfect! I can see that FastAPI inherits from Starlette. Let me create a comprehensive report based on all the information I've gathered:

## Comprehensive FastAPI Middleware Directory Analysis

### 1. **Directory Contents**

The `/fastapi/middleware/` directory contains the following files:

- `__init__.py` - Package initialization
- `cors.py` - CORS middleware
- `gzip.py` - GZip compression middleware
- `httpsredirect.py` - HTTPS redirect middleware
- `trustedhost.py` - Trusted host middleware
- `wsgi.py` - WSGI middleware
- `asyncexitstack.py` - FastAPI-specific async exit stack middleware

### 2. **File Contents**

#### `__init__.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware
```
This file simply re-exports Starlette's Middleware class, making it available directly from `fastapi.middleware`.

#### `cors.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware as CORSMiddleware  # noqa
```
Direct re-export of Starlette's CORSMiddleware, allowing cross-origin resource sharing configuration.

#### `gzip.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.gzip import GZipMiddleware as GZipMiddleware  # noqa
```
Direct re-export of Starlette's GZipMiddleware for response compression.

#### `httpsredirect.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.httpsredirect import (  # noqa
    HTTPSRedirectMiddleware as HTTPSRedirectMiddleware,
)
```
Direct re-export of Starlette's HTTPSRedirectMiddleware for enforcing HTTPS connections.

#### `trustedhost.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.trustedhost import (  # noqa
    TrustedHostMiddleware as TrustedHostMiddleware,
)
```
Direct re-export of Starlette's TrustedHostMiddleware for validating Host headers.

#### `wsgi.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.wsgi import (
    WSGIMiddleware as WSGIMiddleware,
)  # pragma: no cover # noqa
```
Direct re-export of Starlette's WSGIMiddleware for mounting WSGI applications.

#### `asyncexitstack.py` (FastAPI-specific)
```python
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack

from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send


# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
# in their own AsyncExitStack
class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(
        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
    ) -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

This is the **only custom middleware** in FastAPI. It provides a mechanism to manage async context cleanup (primarily for closing files) after a request is completed. It stores an `AsyncExitStack` in the ASGI scope under the key `"fastapi_middleware_astack"`.

### 3. **Middleware Structuring in FastAPI**

Based on the `applications.py` analysis, FastAPI implements middleware as follows:

**Middleware Stack Order (from outermost to innermost):**
1. `ServerErrorMiddleware` - Catches unhandled errors
2. User-provided middleware (via `add_middleware()` or `@app.middleware()`)
3. `ExceptionMiddleware` - Handles FastAPI/Starlette exceptions
4. `AsyncExitStackMiddleware` - Manages async resource cleanup (FastAPI-specific)
5. Router (actual route handlers)

**Key Pattern:**
- Middleware is built in `build_middleware_stack()` method
- Middleware follows the ASGI pattern: `async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send)`
- Middleware wraps applications in reverse order: `for cls, args, kwargs in reversed(middleware):`

### 4. **BaseHTTPMiddleware Pattern**

FastAPI uses Starlette's `BaseHTTPMiddleware` through two mechanisms:

**Via Decorator:**
```python
@app.middleware("http")
async def add_process_time_header(
    request: Request, call_next: Callable[[Request], Awaitable[Response]]
) -> Response:
    start_time = time.time()
    response = await call_next(request)
    process_time = time.time() - start_time
    response.headers["X-Process-Time"] = str(process_time)
    return response
```

**Via Method:**
```python
app.add_middleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware, dispatch=func)
```

The `middleware()` decorator internally converts the request/response signature to `BaseHTTPMiddleware` format and adds it to the middleware stack.

### 5. **FastAPI Architecture Insights**

- **Inheritance**: FastAPI extends Starlette, inheriting the `add_middleware()` method
- **Custom Addition**: FastAPI adds `AsyncExitStackMiddleware` specifically for resource cleanup
- **Context Preservation**: The AsyncExitStackMiddleware placement inside user middlewares preserves contextvars context
- **Re-exports**: All standard Starlette middleware is made available through FastAPI's namespace for convenience

All middleware must implement the ASGI interface (`__call__` method accepting scope, receive, send parameters).

## FastAPI Project Structure - Comprehensive Analysis

Based on my exploration of the FastAPI repository, here's a complete overview:

### 1. **Top-Level Directory Structure**

**Root Directory Contents:**

  - `/scripts` - Build/automation scripts (23 items)

### 5. **Middleware Handling in FastAPI**

**File:** `/fastapi/applications.py` (lines 1022-1070)

#### **Method: `build_middleware_stack()`**

This is the core middleware building method that overrides Starlette's implementation:

```python
def build_middleware_stack(self) -> ASGIApp:
    # Duplicate/override from Starlette to add AsyncExitStackMiddleware
    # inside of ExceptionMiddleware, inside of custom user middlewares
    debug = self.debug
    error_handler = None
    exception_handlers: dict[Any, ExceptionHandler] = {}

    for key, value in self.exception_handlers.items():
        if key in (500, Exception):
            error_handler = value
        else:
            exception_handlers[key] = value

    middleware = (
        [Middleware(ServerErrorMiddleware, handler=error_handler, debug=debug)]
        + self.user_middleware  # Custom user middleware added here
        + [
            Middleware(ExceptionMiddleware, handlers=exception_handlers, debug=debug),
            Middleware(AsyncExitStackMiddleware),  # FastAPI-specific
        ]
    )

    app = self.router
    for cls, args, kwargs in reversed(middleware):
        app = cls(app, *args, **kwargs)
    return app
```

**Middleware Stack Order (innermost to outermost):**
1. `self.router` - The actual route handler
2. `AsyncExitStackMiddleware` - Closes files and async resources
3. `ExceptionMiddleware` - Handles exceptions
4. **User middleware** (in reverse order)
5. `ServerErrorMiddleware` - Handles 500 errors

#### **Method: `middleware()` Decorator** (lines 4658-4702)

```python
@app.middleware("http")
async def add_process_time_header(
    request: Request, 
    call_next: Callable[[Request], Awaitable[Response]]
) -> Response:
    start_time = time.time()
    response = await call_next(request)
    process_time = time.time() - start_time
    response.headers["X-Process-Time"] = str(process_time)
    return response
```

Internally calls: `self.add_middleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware, dispatch=func)`

**Note:** The `add_middleware()` method is **inherited from Starlette** (not overridden in FastAPI).

**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775321641730-0o713y/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`

**Full Contents:**
```python
from pathlib import Path

from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI, File, UploadFile
from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

app = FastAPI()

router = APIRouter()


class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
    Args:
      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
    """

    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size

    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
        received = 0

        async def inner():
            nonlocal received
            message = await receive()
            if message["type"] != "http.request":
                return message  # pragma: no cover

            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
            received += body_len
            if received > self.max_content_size:
                raise HTTPException(
                    422,
                    detail={
                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                        "code": 999,
                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
                    },
                )
            return message

        return inner

    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return

        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)


@router.post("/middleware")
def run_middleware(file: UploadFile = File(..., description="Big File")):
    return {"message": "OK"}


app.include_router(router)
app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)


client = TestClient(app)


def test_custom_middleware_exception(tmp_path: Path):
    default_pydantic_max_size = 2**16
    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
    path.write_bytes(b"x" * (default_pydantic_max_size + 1))

    with client:
        with open(path, "rb") as file:
            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
        assert response.status_code == 422, response.text
        assert response.json() == {
            "detail": {
                "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                "code": 999,
                "message": "File limit exceeded",
            }
        }


def test_custom_middleware_exception_not_raised(tmp_path: Path):
    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
    path.write_bytes(b"<file content>")

    with client:
        with open(path, "rb") as file:
            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
        assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
        assert response.json() == {"message": "OK"}
```

**Key Middleware Patterns:**
- Implements custom ASGI middleware class with `__init__` and `__call__` methods
- Uses `app.add_middleware()` to register middleware
- Middleware intercepts the ASGI scope/receive/send protocol
- Can wrap the receive callable to intercept messages
- Raises HTTPException from middleware to return error responses
- Tests use `tmp_path` pytest fixture for temporary files
- Uses `with client:` context manager for file testing

#### File: `test_exception_handlers.py` (Exception Handler Tests)
**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775321641730-0o713y/tests/test_exception_handlers.py`

**Full Contents (partial - key sections):**
```python
import pytest
from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, HTTPException
from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from starlette.responses import JSONResponse


def http_exception_handler(request, exception):
    return JSONResponse({"exception": "http-exception"})


def request_validation_exception_handler(request, exception):
    return JSONResponse({"exception": "request-validation"})


def server_error_exception_handler(request, exception):
    return JSONResponse(status_code=500, content={"exception": "server-error"})


app = FastAPI(
    exception_handlers={
        HTTPException: http_exception_handler,
        RequestValidationError: request_validation_exception_handler,
        Exception: server_error_exception_handler,
    }
)

client = TestClient(app)


def raise_value_error():
    raise ValueError()


def dependency_with_yield():
    yield raise_value_error()


@app.get("/dependency-with-yield", dependencies=[Depends(dependency_with_yield)])
def with_yield(): ...


@app.get("/http-exception")
def route_with_http_exception():
    raise HTTPException(status_code=400)


@app.get("/request-validation/{param}/")
def route_with_request_validation_exception(param: int):
    pass


@app.get("/server-error")
def route_with_server_error():
    raise RuntimeError("Oops!")


def test_override_http_exception():
    response = client.get("/http-exception")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"exception": "http-exception"}


def test_override_request_validation_exception():
    response = client.get("/request-validation/invalid")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"exception": "request-validation"}


def test_override_server_error_exception_raises():
    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError):
        client.get("/server-error")


def test_override_server_error_exception_response():
    client = TestClient(app, raise_server_exceptions=False)
    response = client.get("/server-error")
    assert response.status_code == 500
    assert response.json() == {"exception": "server-error"}


def test_traceback_for_dependency_with_yield():
    client = TestClient(app, raise_server_exceptions=True)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError) as exc_info:
        client.get("/dependency-with-yield")
    last_frame = exc_info.traceback[-1]
    assert str(last_frame.path) == __file__
    assert last_frame.lineno == raise_value_error.__code__.co_firstlineno
```

**Key Exception Handler Patterns:**
- Passes `exception_handlers` dict to FastAPI constructor
- Maps exception types to handler functions
- Handlers receive `request` and `exception` parameters
- Use `TestClient(app, raise_server_exceptions=False)` to test error handling without raising
- Use `pytest.raises()` context manager to assert exception types
- Can access traceback information via `exc_info.traceback`

### 5. Tutorial-Based Tests

The `test_tutorial/` directory (78 subdirectories) contains tests organized by feature:

**Middleware Example:**
**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775321641730-0o713y/tests/test_tutorial/test_advanced_middleware/test_tutorial001.py`

```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from docs_src.advanced_middleware.tutorial001_py310 import app

def test_middleware():
    client = TestClient(app, base_url="https://testserver")
    response = client.get("/")
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text

    client = TestClient(app)
    response = client.get("/", follow_redirects=False)
    assert response.status_code == 307, response.text
    assert response.headers["location"] == "https://testserver/"
```

**Tutorial Test Patterns:**
- Import app from `docs_src` package (documentation source code examples)
- Use `TestClient(app, base_url="...")` to set base URL
- Use `follow_redirects=False` to test redirect responses
- Test access to response headers via `response.headers`

### 6. Test Configuration (from pyproject.toml)

**Pytest Configuration:**
```toml
[tool.pytest]
minversion = "9.0"
addopts = [
  "--strict-config",
  "--strict-markers",
  "--ignore=docs_src",
]
strict_xfail = true
filterwarnings = ["error"]
timeout = "20"
```

**Test Dependencies:**
- httpx (>= 0.23.0)
- pytest (>= 9.0.0)
- pytest-codspeed (>= 4.2.0)
- pytest-xdist (for parallel testing)
- pytest-cov (coverage)
- pytest-sugar (better output)
- pytest-timeout (20 second timeout)

### 7. Key Testing Patterns Identified

1. **TestClient Usage:**
   - All tests use `fastapi.testclient.TestClient`
   - Client initialized with app instance
   - Supports HTTP methods: `get()`, `post()`, `put()`, `delete()`, etc.
   - Supports WebSocket: `websocket_connect()`
   - Parameters: `base_url`, `follow_redirects`, `raise_server_exceptions`

2. **Response Assertions:**
   - Check `response.status_code`
   - Check `response.json()` for JSON responses
   - Check `response.text` for text responses
   - Access `response.headers` for header inspection

3. **Middleware Testing:**
   - Custom middleware implements ASGI `__call__` protocol
   - Register via `app.add_middleware()`
   - Wrap scope/receive/send callables
   - Can raise HTTPException from middleware

4. **Exception Handling:**
   - Custom exception handlers in FastAPI constructor
   - Use `pytest.raises()` for exception assertions
   - TestClient option: `raise_server_exceptions`

5. **File/Form Testing:**
   - `files={"name": file}` parameter for multipart uploads
   - Use pytest `tmp_path` fixture for temporary files
   - Can use context manager: `with client:`

6. **Parameterization:**
   - Use `@pytest.mark.parametrize()` for multiple test cases
   - Reduces code duplication

### 8. File Paths Summary

**Key Files Located:**
- Root test files: 204 test_*.py files in `/tests/`
- Middleware test: `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`
- Exception handlers: `/tests/test_exception_handlers.py`
- Query params: `/tests/test_query.py`
- Route scope: `/tests/test_route_scope.py`
- Application tests: `/tests/test_application.py`
- Tutorial tests: `/tests/test_tutorial/` (78 subdirectories)
- Config: `/pyproject.toml` with pytest configuration
- Shared app: `/tests/main.py` (used by multiple test files)

This comprehensive test structure demonstrates professional testing practices with extensive coverage of middleware, exception handling, request validation, and various FastAPI features.

```python
from pathlib import Path

from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI, File, UploadFile
from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

app = FastAPI()

router = APIRouter()


class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
    Args:
      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
    """

    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size

    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
        received = 0

        async def inner():
            nonlocal received
            message = await receive()
            if message["type"] != "http.request":
                return message  # pragma: no cover

            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
            received += body_len
            if received > self.max_content_size:
                raise HTTPException(
                    422,
                    detail={
                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                        "code": 999,
                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
                    },
                )
            return message

        return inner

    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return

        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)


@router.post("/middleware")
def run_middleware(file: UploadFile = File(..., description="Big File")):
    return {"message": "OK"}


app.include_router(router)
app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)

        assert response.json() == {
            "detail": {
                "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                "code": 999,
                "message": "File limit exceeded",
            }
        }


def test_custom_middleware_exception_not_raised(tmp_path: Path):
    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
    path.write_bytes(b"<file content>")

    with client:
        with open(path, "rb") as file:
            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})

        assert response.json() == {"message": "OK"}
```

```python
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"exception": "http-exception"}


def test_override_request_validation_exception():
    response = client.get("/request-validation/invalid")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"exception": "request-validation"}


def test_override_server_error_exception_raises():
    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError):
        client.get("/server-error")


def test_override_server_error_exception_response():
    client = TestClient(app, raise_server_exceptions=False)
    response = client.get("/server-error")
    assert response.status_code == 500
    assert response.json() == {"exception": "server-error"}


def test_traceback_for_dependency_with_yield():
    client = TestClient(app, raise_server_exceptions=True)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError) as exc_info:
        client.get("/dependency-with-yield")
    last_frame = exc_info.traceback[-1]
    assert str(last_frame.path) == __file__
    assert last_frame.lineno == raise_value_error.__code__.co_firstlineno
```

**Key Exception Handler Patterns:**
- Passes `exception_handlers` dict to FastAPI constructor
- Maps exception types to handler functions
- Handlers receive `request` and `exception` parameters
- Use `TestClient(app, raise_server_exceptions=False)` to test error handling without raising
- Use `pytest.raises()` context manager to assert exception types
- Can access traceback information via `exc_info.traceback`

### 5. Tutorial-Based Tests

The `test_tutorial/` directory (78 subdirectories) contains tests organized by feature:

**Middleware Example:**
**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775321641730-0o713y/tests/test_tutorial/test_advanced_middleware/test_tutorial001.py`

```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from docs_src.advanced_middleware.tutorial001_py310 import app

def test_middleware():
    client = TestClient(app, base_url="https://testserver")
    response = client.get("/")
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text

    client = TestClient(app)
    response = client.get("/", follow_redirects=False)
    assert response.status_code == 307, response.text
    assert response.headers["location"] == "https://testserver/"
```

**Tutorial Test Patterns:**
- Import app from `docs_src` package (documentation source code examples)
- Use `TestClient(app, base_url="...")` to set base URL
- Use `follow_redirects=False` to test redirect responses
- Test access to response headers via `response.headers`

### 6. Test Configuration (from pyproject.toml)

**Pytest Configuration:**
```toml
[tool.pytest]
minversion = "9.0"
addopts = [
  "--strict-config",
  "--strict-markers",
  "--ignore=docs_src",
]
strict_xfail = true
filterwarnings = ["error"]
timeout = "20"
```

**Test Dependencies:**
- httpx (>= 0.23.0)
- pytest (>= 9.0.0)
- pytest-codspeed (>= 4.2.0)
- pytest-xdist (for parallel testing)
- pytest-cov (coverage)
- pytest-sugar (better output)
- pytest-timeout (20 second timeout)

### 7. Key Testing Patterns Identified

1. **TestClient Usage:**
   - All tests use `fastapi.testclient.TestClient`
   - Client initialized with app instance
   - Supports HTTP methods: `get()`, `post()`, `put()`, `delete()`, etc.
   - Supports WebSocket: `websocket_connect()`
   - Parameters: `base_url`, `follow_redirects`, `raise_server_exceptions`

2. **Response Assertions:**
   - Check `response.status_code`
   - Check `response.json()` for JSON responses
   - Check `response.text` for text responses
   - Access `response.headers` for header inspection

3. **Middleware Testing:**
   - Custom middleware implements ASGI `__call__` protocol
   - Register via `app.add_middleware()`
   - Wrap scope/receive/send callables
   - Can raise HTTPException from middleware

4. **Exception Handling:**
   - Custom exception handlers in FastAPI constructor
   - Use `pytest.raises()` for exception assertions
   - TestClient option: `raise_server_exceptions`

5. **File/Form Testing:**
   - `files={"name": file}` parameter for multipart uploads
   - Use pytest `tmp_path` fixture for temporary files
   - Can use context manager: `with client:`

6. **Parameterization:**
   - Use `@pytest.mark.parametrize()` for multiple test cases
   - Reduces code duplication

### 8. File Paths Summary

**Key Files Located:**
- Root test files: 204 test_*.py files in `/tests/`
- Middleware test: `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`
- Exception handlers: `/tests/test_exception_handlers.py`
- Query params: `/tests/test_query.py`
- Route scope: `/tests/test_route_scope.py`
- Application tests: `/tests/test_application.py`
- Tutorial tests: `/tests/test_tutorial/` (78 subdirectories)
- Config: `/pyproject.toml` with pytest configuration
- Shared app: `/tests/main.py` (used by multiple test files)

This comprehensive test structure demonstrates professional testing practices with extensive coverage of middleware, exception handling, request validation, and various FastAPI features.

Design a detailed implementation plan for a rate limiting middleware for FastAPI. Here's everything I know about the codebase:

## Codebase Context

**`fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`** contains only:
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware
```

**`fastapi/middleware/asyncexitstack.py`** — the ONLY custom FastAPI middleware, follows raw ASGI pattern:
```python
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack
from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send

class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack") -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

All other middleware files (cors.py, gzip.py, httpsredirect.py, trustedhost.py) are single-line re-exports from Starlette.

**Middleware stack order** (FastAPI's `build_middleware_stack`):
1. ServerErrorMiddleware (outermost)
2. User middleware (via add_middleware)
3. ExceptionMiddleware
4. AsyncExitStackMiddleware
5. Router (innermost)

Middleware is registered with: `app.add_middleware(MyMiddlewareClass, param1=value1, ...)`

**Tests** use `fastapi.testclient.TestClient`. Test files define apps inline and use `pytest`. No `tests/test_apps/` directory exists — it must be created.

**Test pattern** (from `test_custom_middleware_exception.py`):
```python
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

app = FastAPI()
app.add_middleware(SomeMiddleware, max_content_size=256)

client = TestClient(app)

def test_something():
    response = client.get("/endpoint")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"message": "OK"}
```

**pyproject.toml pytest config**: `filterwarnings = ["error"]` — warnings become errors.

## Requirements

Create `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py` with:
1. `RateLimitMiddleware` class following raw ASGI pattern (like asyncexitstack.py)
2. `__init__` params: `app: ASGIApp`, `requests_per_minute: int = 60`, `burst_size: int = 10`
3. Token bucket algorithm for rate limiting per client IP
4. Returns HTTP 429 with JSON `{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded", "retry_after": <seconds>}` when exceeded
5. Adds to ALL responses:
   - `X-RateLimit-Limit`: requests_per_minute
   - `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: remaining tokens (integer)
   - `X-RateLimit-Reset`: seconds until bucket is full again
6. Export from `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`

Also create `tests/test_apps/test_rate_limit_app.py` demonstrating usage + tests.

## Key Design Questions to Address

1. **Header injection on ALL responses**: Use send wrapper that intercepts `http.response.start` messages and uses `starlette.datastructures.MutableHeaders(scope=message)` to add headers
2. **Token bucket state**: Per-instance dict `{ip: [tokens, last_refill_time]}` with `asyncio.Lock()` for thread safety
3. **Client IP extraction**: Check `x-forwarded-for` header first, fall back to `scope["client"][0]`
4. **retry_after calculation**: `math.ceil((1.0 - remaining_tokens) / refill_rate)` when rejected
5. **X-RateLimit-Reset**: `math.ceil((burst_size - remaining_tokens) / refill_rate)` seconds until bucket full
6. **Non-HTTP scopes**: Pass through immediately (websockets, lifespan)

## Specific Items Needed in Plan

1. Complete `rate_limit.py` file structure with all imports
2. Exact token bucket algorithm with asyncio.Lock pattern
3. How to intercept the `send` callable to add headers
4. How `MutableHeaders` is used to modify response headers in ASGI
5. The export line to add to `__init__.py`
6. Complete test file structure for `tests/test_apps/test_rate_limit_app.py` including:
   - Tests for normal request success + headers present
   - Tests for 429 when rate limit exceeded (use small burst_size like 2)
   - Tests for 429 response body
   - Tests for rate recovery after time passes (mock `time.monotonic`)
   - Whether `tests/test_apps/__init__.py` is needed

Please provide a detailed step-by-step implementation plan with actual code examples for each component.

1	from pathlib import Path
2	
3	from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI, File, UploadFile
4	from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
5	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
6	
7	app = FastAPI()
8	
9	router = APIRouter()
10	
11	
12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54	
55	
56	@router.post("/middleware")
57	def run_middleware(file: UploadFile = File(..., description="Big File")):
58	    return {"message": "OK"}
59	
60	
61	app.include_router(router)
62	app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)
63	
64	
65	client = TestClient(app)
66	
67	
68	def test_custom_middleware_exception(tmp_path: Path):
69	    default_pydantic_max_size = 2**16
70	    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
71	    path.write_bytes(b"x" * (default_pydantic_max_size + 1))
72	
73	    with client:
74	        with open(path, "rb") as file:
75	            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
76	        assert response.status_code == 422, response.text
77	        assert response.json() == {
78	            "detail": {
79	                "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
80	                "code": 999,
81	                "message": "File limit exceeded",
82	            }
83	        }
84	
85	
86	def test_custom_middleware_exception_not_raised(tmp_path: Path):
87	    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
88	    path.write_bytes(b"<file content>")
89	
90	    with client:
91	        with open(path, "rb") as file:
92	            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
93	        assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
94	        assert response.json() == {"message": "OK"}
95

1	import time
2	import pytest
3	from fastapi import FastAPI
4	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
5	from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware
6	
7	
8	@pytest.fixture
9	def app():
10	    app = FastAPI()
11	    app.add_middleware(
12	        RateLimitMiddleware,
13	        requests_per_minute=60,
14	        burst_size=5,
15	    )
16	
17	    @app.get("/")
18	    async def root():
19	        return {"message": "hello"}
20	
21	    @app.get("/other")
22	    async def other():
23	        return {"message": "other"}
24	
25	    return app
26	
27	
28	@pytest.fixture
29	def client(app):
30	    return TestClient(app)
31	
32	
33	class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
34	    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
35	        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
36	        for _ in range(5):
37	            response = client.get("/")
38	            assert response.status_code == 200
39	
40	    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
41	        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
42	        # Use up the burst
43	        for _ in range(5):
44	            client.get("/")
45	        # Next request should be rate limited
46	        response = client.get("/")
47	        assert response.status_code == 429
48	        body = response.json()
49	        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
50	        assert "retry_after" in body
51	
52	    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
53	        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
54	        response = client.get("/")
55	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
56	        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
57	        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers
58	
59	    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
60	        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
61	        r1 = client.get("/")
62	        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
63	        r2 = client.get("/")
64	        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
65	        assert remaining2 < remaining1
66	
67	    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
68	        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
69	        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
70	        response = client.get("/")
71	        assert response.status_code == 200
72	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
73	        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"
74	
75	    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
76	        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
77	        # Use up burst across different endpoints
78	        for _ in range(3):
79	            client.get("/")
80	        for _ in range(2):
81	            client.get("/other")
82	        # Should be rate limited now
83	        response = client.get("/")
84	        assert response.status_code == 429
85

1	"""
2	Test that async streaming endpoints can be cancelled without hanging.
3	
4	Ref: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/issues/14680
5	"""
6	
7	from collections.abc import AsyncIterable
8	
9	import anyio
10	import pytest
11	from fastapi import FastAPI
12	from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
13	
14	pytestmark = [
15	    pytest.mark.anyio,
16	    pytest.mark.filterwarnings("ignore::pytest.PytestUnraisableExceptionWarning"),
17	]
18	
19	
20	app = FastAPI()
21	
22	
23	@app.get("/stream-raw", response_class=StreamingResponse)
24	async def stream_raw() -> AsyncIterable[str]:
25	    """Async generator with no internal await - would hang without checkpoint."""
26	    i = 0
27	    while True:
28	        yield f"item {i}\n"
29	        i += 1
30	
31	
32	@app.get("/stream-jsonl")
33	async def stream_jsonl() -> AsyncIterable[int]:
34	    """JSONL async generator with no internal await."""
35	    i = 0
36	    while True:
37	        yield i
38	        i += 1
39	
40	
41	async def _run_asgi_and_cancel(app: FastAPI, path: str, timeout: float) -> bool:
42	    """Call the ASGI app for *path* and cancel after *timeout* seconds.
43	
44	    Returns `True` if the cancellation was delivered (i.e. it did not hang).
45	    """
46	    chunks: list[bytes] = []
47	
48	    async def receive():  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
49	        # Simulate a client that never disconnects, rely on cancellation
50	        await anyio.sleep(float("inf"))
51	        return {"type": "http.disconnect"}  # pragma: no cover
52	
53	    async def send(message: dict) -> None:  # type: ignore[type-arg]
54	        if message["type"] == "http.response.body":
55	            chunks.append(message.get("body", b""))
56	
57	    scope = {
58	        "type": "http",
59	        "asgi": {"version": "3.0", "spec_version": "2.0"},
60	        "http_version": "1.1",
61	        "method": "GET",
62	        "path": path,
63	        "query_string": b"",
64	        "root_path": "",
65	        "headers": [],
66	        "server": ("test", 80),
67	    }
68	
69	    with anyio.move_on_after(timeout) as cancel_scope:
70	        await app(scope, receive, send)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
71	
72	    # If we got here within the timeout the generator was cancellable.
73	    # cancel_scope.cancelled_caught is True when move_on_after fired.
74	    return cancel_scope.cancelled_caught or len(chunks) > 0
75	
76	
77	async def test_raw_stream_cancellation() -> None:
78	    """Raw streaming endpoint should be cancellable within a reasonable time."""
79	    cancelled = await _run_asgi_and_cancel(app, "/stream-raw", timeout=3.0)
80	    # The key assertion: we reached this line at all (didn't hang).
81	    # cancelled will be True because the infinite generator was interrupted.
82	    assert cancelled
83	
84	
85	async def test_jsonl_stream_cancellation() -> None:
86	    """JSONL streaming endpoint should be cancellable within a reasonable time."""
87	    cancelled = await _run_asgi_and_cancel(app, "/stream-jsonl", timeout=3.0)
88	    assert cancelled
89

26	    )

30	    websocket: WebSocket, exc: WebSocketRequestValidationError
31	) -> None:
32	    await websocket.close(
33	        code=WS_1008_POLICY_VIOLATION, reason=jsonable_encoder(exc.errors())

21	class UploadFile(StarletteUploadFile):
22	    """
23	    A file uploaded in a request.
24	
25	    Define it as a *path operation function* (or dependency) parameter.
26	
27	    If you are using a regular `def` function, you can use the `upload_file.file`
28	    attribute to access the raw standard Python file (blocking, not async), useful and
29	    needed for non-async code.
30	
31	    Read more about it in the
32	    [FastAPI docs for Request Files](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/request-files/).
33	
34	    ## Example
35	
36	    ```python
37	    from typing import Annotated
38	
39	    from fastapi import FastAPI, File, UploadFile
40	
41	    app = FastAPI()
42	
43	
44	    @app.post("/files/")
45	    async def create_file(file: Annotated[bytes, File()]):
46	        return {"file_size": len(file)}
47	
48	
49	    @app.post("/uploadfile/")
50	    async def create_upload_file(file: UploadFile):
51	        return {"filename": file.filename}
52	    ```
53	    """
54	
55	    file: Annotated[
56	        BinaryIO,
57	        Doc("The standard Python file object (non-async)."),
58	    ]
59	    filename: Annotated[str | None, Doc("The original file name.")]
60	    size: Annotated[int | None, Doc("The size of the file in bytes.")]
61	    headers: Annotated[Headers, Doc("The headers of the request.")]
62	    content_type: Annotated[
63	        str | None, Doc("The content type of the request, from the headers.")
64	    ]
65	
66	    async def write(
67	        self,
68	        data: Annotated[
69	            bytes,
70	            Doc(
71	                """
72	                The bytes to write to the file.
73	                """
74	            ),
75	        ],
76	    ) -> None:
77	        """
78	        Write some bytes to the file.
79	
80	        You normally wouldn't use this from a file you read in a request.
81	
82	        To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.
83	        """
84	        return await super().write(data)
85	
86	    async def read(
87	        self,
88	        size: Annotated[
89	            int,
90	            Doc(
91	                """
92	                The number of bytes to read from the file.
93	                """
94	            ),
95	        ] = -1,
96	    ) -> bytes:
97	        """
98	        Read some bytes from the file.
99	
100	        To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.
101	        """
102	        return await super().read(size)
103	
104	    async def seek(
105	        self,
106	        offset: Annotated[
107	            int,
108	            Doc(
109	                """
110	                The position in bytes to seek to in the file.
111	                """
112	            ),
113	        ],
114	    ) -> None:
115	        """
116	        Move to a position in the file.
117	
118	        Any next read or write will be done from that position.
119	
120	        To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.
121	        """
122	        return await super().seek(offset)
123	
124	    async def close(self) -> None:
125	        """
126	        Close the file.
127	
128	        To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.
129	        """
130	        return await super().close()
131	
132	    @classmethod
133	    def _validate(cls, __input_value: Any, _: Any) -> "UploadFile":
134	        if not isinstance(__input_value, StarletteUploadFile):
135	            raise ValueError(f"Expected UploadFile, received: {type(__input_value)}")
136	        return cast(UploadFile, __input_value)
137

140	        cls, core_schema: Mapping[str, Any], handler: GetJsonSchemaHandler
141	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:

146	        cls, source: type[Any], handler: Callable[[Any], Mapping[str, Any]]
147	    ) -> Mapping[str, Any]:

153	class DefaultPlaceholder:
154	    """
155	    You shouldn't use this class directly.
156	
157	    It's used internally to recognize when a default value has been overwritten, even
158	    if the overridden default value was truthy.
159	    """
160

162	        self.value = value

165	        return bool(self.value)
166	
167	    def __eq__(self, o: object) -> bool:
168	        return isinstance(o, DefaultPlaceholder) and o.value == self.value
